subject: [PATCH v6 3/6] fuse: add io-uring buffer pools

2026-07-16.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new feature to the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) implementation over io-uring, allowing the server to pass in a buffer pool that the kernel can use for servicing requests. This decouples the relationship between entries and payload buffers, enabling the kernel to optimize memory usage and reduce requirements for using fuse-over-io-uring. The patch adds support for registering a buffer pool per queue with the FUSE_IO_URING_CMD_ADD_BUFPOOL command, which is used by the server to pass in a contiguous chunk of memory. The kernel then splits this region into buffers based on the maximum payload size and tracks free buffers with a bitmap. On dispatch, requests that need a payload claim a free buffer, and on completion, the buffer is returned to the pool or reused directly if needed again.
